channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Australian police link stash of explosives and list of Jewish targets to an antisemitic plot
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australian police believe explosives found on Sydney’s outskirts were evidence of a deadly escalation in a campaign of antisemitic arson and graffiti crimes that has been waged in major cities for months, officials said on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Year of the Snake is underway with Lunar New Year festivities around the world
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — Firecrackers popped, incense was offered at temples and dancers and drummers paraded Wednesday in Asia and farther afield as millions around the world celebrated the Lunar New Year.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Police say at least 30 people have died in a stampede at the massive Maha Kumbh festival in India
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — At least 30 people were killed and many more injured in a stampede at the world’s largest religious gathering early Wednesday, police said, as millions of pilgrims rushed to dip in sacred waters during the Maha Kumbh festival in northern India.
item.dc:creator: RAJESH KUMAR SINGH and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: M23 rebels expand their control of Goma in eastern Congo
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Rwanda-backed rebels captured large parts of Goma, the biggest city in eastern Congo, including its airport, the United Nations said, as Rwanda's president on Wednesday joined calls for a ceasefire in the decadeslong conflict amid mounting international pressure.
item.dc:creator: CHINEDU ASADU and JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Western officials warn Ukraine over weapons procurement dispute
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Western officials have warned Ukraine that an escalating rift between the defense minister and procurement chief may jeopardize trust in the country and urged the government to resolve the situation quickly so as not to disrupt weapons supplies.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Scholz's challenger brings tough migration plans to German parliament, faces blowback on far right
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz's main challenger in Germany's upcoming election plans to put proposals for a tougher migration policy to parliament on Wednesday, a maneuver aimed at piling pressure on the governing parties that has brought accusations that he's breaking commitments to shun the
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US watchdog launches database on Catholic priests accused of sex abuse of minors in the Philippines
item.description: A United States-based watchdog has launched an online database on more than 80 Roman Catholic priests who have been accused of sexually abusing minors in the Philippines and said the silence of Filipino bishops on the such assaults amounted to a cover-up.
item.dc:creator: Jim Gomez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: 14 members of an Australian religious group are convicted over diabetic girl's death
item.description: BRISBANE, Australia (AP) — Two bereaved parents and 12 fellow members of an Australian religious congregation accused of killing an 8-year-old girl by withholding her diabetes medication were found guilty on Wednesday of manslaughter. Elizabeth Rose Struhs died on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Maha Kumbh festival in northern India is the world's largest religious gathering
item.description: PRAYAGRAJ, India (AP) — Hindu devotees, mystics and holy men and women have gathered in India's northern city of Prayagraj for the Maha Kumbh festival, the world's largest religious gathering. The festival is held every 12 years at the confluence of sacred rivers.
item.dc:creator: SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli father says even in Hamas captivity, his daughter heard his pleas to come home alive
item.description: For more than 470 days, Eitan Gonen publicly pleaded with his daughter to stay alive while in Hamas captivity.
item.dc:creator: Sam Mednick, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The discovery of brutal mass graves in Syria reveals Assad's legacy of horror
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — The charred remains of at least 26 victims of the Bashar Assad government were located Tuesday by Syrian civil defense workers in two separate basements in rural Damascus.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM AND SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US ships return to Red Sea amid Gaza ceasefire
item.description: U.S. ships are returning to the Red Sea following promises from Yemen’s Houthi rebels to abstain from attacks on American and British vessels. The pledge, which comes after more than a year of shipping traffic disruption on a major international trade route, was made on Jan.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Serbia's prime minister resigns as anti-corruption protests sparked by deadly collapse swell
item.description: BELGRADE, Serbia (AP) — Serbia’s populist prime minister, Milos Vucevic, resigned Tuesday in an attempt to calm political tensions stoked by weeks of massive anti-corruption protests that started after the deadly collapse of a concrete overhang at a recently renovated train station.
item.dc:creator: JOVANA GEC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats block GOP's ICC sanctions bill
item.description: Senate Democrats on Tuesday blocked Republicans from advancing legislation to sanction the International Criminal Court (ICC), arguing the bill had sweeping consequences on allies and American businesses.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Amazon's advocates fear Trump's return means little US help to protect rainforest
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Alexis Damancio Silva can’t forget the hardship in his town of Puerto Narino in far southern Colombia last year when extreme drought nearly dried up the Amazon River. Pink dolphins and fish died. Crops dried up. The town lost its easy access to markets.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italy sends 49 more migrants to Albania for processing following earlier court rejections
item.description: SHENGJIN, Albania (AP) — An Italian navy ship arrived Tuesday in Albania with 49 migrants intercepted in international waters for processing of their asylum applications at special Albanian centers, in the third such attempt following hurdles earlier raised by Italian courts.
item.dc:creator: ERION XHABAFTI and LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trains canceled across Bangladesh as rail union goes on strike
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Trains were canceled across Bangladesh on Tuesday as railway staff went on strike for higher pensions and other benefits, affecting tens of thousands of passengers and freight transport.
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: CDC ordered to stop working with WHO immediately, upending expectations of an extended withdrawal
item.description: President Donald Trump last week issued an executive order to begin the process of withdrawing the U.S. from WHO, but that did not take immediate effect.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump speaks with India's Modi about trade, future White House visit
item.description: President Trump spoke Monday with Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi, discussing the relationship between their two countries and plans for Modi to visit the White House.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: White House: Colombia to take deported migrants after Trump showdown
item.description: The White House said a U.S.-Colombia agreement had come together in the wake of a back-and-forth over immigration and tariffs.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: A child's life in Gaza, as explained by a Middle East reporter
item.description: Middle East reporter Arwa Damon, who founded the nonprofit INARA, described what she's seen in Gaza to NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: In ‘Hail Mary’ move, ICC prosecutor files surprise application as Senate set to vote on sanctioning court
item.description: As the ICC prosecutor files applications for warrants against the Taliban, the Senate is set to vote on sanctions in bill that would trigger action among other things if US citizens, allies are prosecuted.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Holocaust Remembrance Day: A town once inhabited by Nazis reconciles with the past
item.description: As Holocaust Remembrance Day is marked on Jan. 27, a town in southwestern Germany unflinchingly confronts its past and reaches out to Jews.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump wants Egypt and Jordan to take in Palestinians from Gaza. Here's why they are likely to refuse
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — President Donald Trump's suggestion that Egypt and Jordan take in Palestinians from the war-ravaged Gaza Strip is likely to be met with a hard “no" from the two U.S. allies and the Palestinians themselves, who fear Israel would never allow them to return.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Auschwitz 80 years since liberation: Ryszard Horowitz's story of survival and making the American Dream
item.description: An exclusive interview with Auschwitz survivor and award-winning photographer Ryszard Horowitz, on his extraordinary life and his perilous childhood as a Jewish boy in Nazi-occupied Kraków.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump: Make products in America or face tariffs
item.description: “Come make your product in America, and we will give you among the lowest taxes of any nation on earth,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Tabloids apologize to Prince Harry, admit intruding on Princess Diana
item.description: Prince Harry claimed a monumental victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
